title: Progress Bar
description: Progress Bar visualizes completion for uploads, processing, quotas, and multi-step work. Use determinate mode when you know the percentage and indeterminate mode when work is active but duration is unknown.
intro: Progress Bar renders a compact linear indicator with semantic variants, three sizes, and determinate or indeterminate behavior. Pair it with nearby text so users understand what is progressing and what happens next.

featureSections.title: Basic determinate progress
featureSections.description: Use determinate mode when the system can report a real percentage. Keep a short label nearby so the bar is not just visual decoration.

featureSections.title: Determinate and indeterminate states
featureSections.description: Switch to indeterminate when work is active but exact completion is unknown, then return to determinate once a real percentage is available again.

featureSections.title: Sizes and density
featureSections.description: Small fits dense tables and rows, medium is the default for forms and panels, and large gives more visual weight in banners or prominent status areas.

featureSections.title: Semantic variants
featureSections.description: Color should communicate status, not decoration: success for completion, warning for near-limit states, danger for failure, and info or primary for active work.

featureSections.title: Upload queue pattern
featureSections.description: A queue with filenames, percentages, and mixed statuses is more useful than isolated bars because it mirrors a real product surface users actually need to build.

featureSections.title: Task flow with actions
featureSections.description: Combine progress, status copy, and actions in one panel so users can retry, continue, or wait without hunting for context elsewhere.

accessibility.title: Role and value
accessibility.body:
`ProgressBarComponent` renders with `role="progressbar"`. In determinate mode it exposes `aria-valuenow`, `aria-valuemin`, and `aria-valuemax`; in indeterminate mode those numeric value attributes are omitted.

| Mode | Accessibility behavior |
| --- | --- |
| determinate | exposes current value and range |
| indeterminate | omits numeric value attributes |

accessibility.title: Accessible name
accessibility.body: Use `ariaLabel` to describe what is progressing, for example `Uploading contract.pdf` or `Storage usage`. The default label is only a generic fallback.

accessibility.title: Value text
accessibility.body:
Use `ariaValueText` when percent alone is not enough, such as `Step 2 of 4`, `3 files remaining`, or `Waiting for virus scan`.

| State | Attribute / guidance |
| --- | --- |
| current progress | `aria-valuenow` when determinate |
| min / max | `aria-valuemin` and `aria-valuemax` |
| richer wording | `aria-valuetext` or adjacent status text |

apiSections.title: ProgressBarComponent - ui-progress-bar
apiSections.lead: Standalone linear progress indicator for known percentages and unknown-duration work. The component stays intentionally compact, so surrounding text should carry most of the task meaning.

blocks.title: Inputs
rows: Applies the semantic track color. Use it to communicate task state rather than to decorate the page.
Controls bar thickness for dense rows versus more prominent status surfaces.
Determinate expects a real value; indeterminate shows ongoing work with animated motion and no numeric completion.
Used in determinate mode. Values are clamped into the 0 to 100 range before rendering and before ARIA exposure.
Accessible name for the progress bar. When empty, the component falls back to the shared i18n progress label.
Optional human-readable status text when percent alone is not descriptive enough.

paragraphs: In indeterminate mode the component omits aria-valuenow, aria-valuemin, and aria-valuemax because exact completion is unknown.
The visual track width is driven only by value in determinate mode. Indeterminate mode ignores width input and uses the built-in animated segment.
blocks.title: Notes
Related mechanisms
items: Variant and Size - shared unions used across surfaces and controls.
Shared i18n service - provides the fallback accessible label when ariaLabel is not supplied.
